Title: Discovery of FoTO1 and Taxol genes enables biosynthesis of baccatin III

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FoTO1, an NTF2-like protein ranked 13 in Taxol module 1, increased yields from early Taxol pathways by 10–17-fold in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. FoTO1 suppressed T5αH-derived rearranged side products and promoted formation of taxadien-5α-ol and downstream on-pathway products. 3. FoTO1 binds T5αH and TDS2 with high-nanomolar affinity, and the C-terminal helix is required for both binding and metabolic phenotype. 4. FoTO1 also streamlines T13αH-mediated oxidation, facilitating taxadien-5α,13α-diol formation and partially compensating for T5αH.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; bulk RNA-seq comparison: PRJNA493167; PRJNA251671; PRJNA733140; PRJNA427840; PRJNA497542; PRJNA499080; PRJNA864083; metabolomics by GC-MS and LC-MS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Paclitaxel/Taxol is an anti-cancer therapeutic and microtubule-stabilizing agent; baccatin III is the industrial precursor for semi-synthesis of Taxol.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FoTO1 is an unanticipated non-enzymatic pathway factor. A Taxus paralogue and the Arabidopsis homologue did not reproduce the FoTO1 metabolic phenotype. Protein localizations differ among partners: TDS plastid, T5αH ER membrane, FoTO1 cytoplasm, suggesting membrane-contact-site-associated scaffolding/organization.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; truncation and mutagenesis analysis; microscale thermophoresis; co-immunoprecipitation; subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes productive early taxane oxidation and suppresses T5αH/T13αH side-product formation, thereby increasing flux toward baccatin III and late Taxol intermediates.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Multiplexed elicitation of Taxus needles with hormones, microorganisms and other elicitors; chitosan and methyl jasmonate increased early pathway metabolite accumulation contextually; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed in Taxol module 1, associated with elicited cell states and early Taxol biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory factor for metabolic engineering of paclitaxel/taxane production because it resolves the long-standing first-oxidation bottleneck and should be co-expressed with early pathway genes.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4O3A intermediate 5; ultimately supports taxusin formation with TAX19
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a C-9α hydroxyl into a mid-pathway taxane intermediate in the C-13α-hydroxyl branch, enabling subsequent acetylation and downstream Taxol-pathway progression.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-selective step; required enzyme choice for productive baccatin III reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for semi-synthesis of the anti-cancer drug Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct downstream intermediate on the productive baccatin III branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T9αH-750C, a CYP750C P450 ranked 27 in module 1, depleted 3O2A intermediate 4 and generated new 4O3A intermediate 5. 2. Structural characterization of downstream products supported its assignment as the taxane 9α-hydroxylase. 3. Unlike T9αH-725A, T9αH-750C functions on the C-13α-hydroxyl branch compatible with Taxol/baccatin III biosynthesis and was used in all productive reconstitutions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents independently evolved T9αH activity distinct from CYP725A-family T9αH-725A; T9αH-725A appears specific for C-13α-acetoxy taxanes and was incompatible with productive baccatin III synthesis in this system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; TAX19-assisted product trapping; NMR and MS/MS structural characterization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alternative T9αH enzymes with different substrate specificity can divert flux to other taxane branches.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes productive flux toward baccatin III by catalyzing the relevant C-9α hydroxylation on the Taxol-compatible branch.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred T9αH for heterologous baccatin III/Taxol-pathway engineering; should replace incompatible T9αH-725A in productive constructs.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/GC-MS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mid-late oxygenated taxane branch preceding oxetane formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates the C-7β hydroxyl of mid-pathway taxanes, including acetylation, generating substrates competent for subsequent TOT-mediated oxetane/epoxide formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key prerequisite step for TOT function
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream modification of immediate precursors toward end product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T7AT, the 15th gene in module 2, catalysed various C-7β-O-acylations including acetylation. 2. Addition of T7AT generated 8-Ac and 8-Bz from 6O4A intermediates. 3. In the absence of T7AT, TOT showed little or no productive conversion, demonstrating that C-7β acylation is required for efficient TOT function.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Independently reported elsewhere, but this study established its importance as a prerequisite for TOT activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling with/without TOT
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Adds a transient acyl group later removed by downstream deacetylation; omission blocks productive oxetane pathway progression.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables downstream TOT-dependent formation of highly oxygenated taxanes on the route to baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential inclusion for heterologous Taxol-pathway engineering, despite generating a transient protective acylation that must later be removed.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxygenated taxane branch toward baccatin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 7O5A/7O6A oxygenated taxane intermediates including taxusin-derived and benzoylated congeners
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 1β-hydroxylated products including baccatin VI (11-Bz) and 1β-hydroxytaxusin; also rearranged abeo-taxane product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses C-1β hydroxylation of highly oxygenated taxanes; can also yield rearranged 11(15→1)abeo-taxane products associated with radical rearrangement.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing late oxygenation step previously unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct late intermediates on route to baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T1βH-184, a 2-ODD in module 2, produced mono-oxidized products when added to advanced pathway intermediates. 2. With TAX19 and upstream pathways, it yielded baccatin VI (11-Bz), supporting assignment as the missing taxane 1β-hydroxylase. 3. Structural characterization revealed both 1β-hydroxytaxusin and rearranged 15-hydroxy-11(15→1)abeo-taxusin, indicating dual catalytic outcomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalysis can produce off-pathway/rearranged abeo-taxanes; less favorable than T1βH-686 for desired 10-Bz accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression and feeding assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS; purification and NMR structural characterization of products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produces rearranged abeo-taxane side products, potentially reducing flux to desired end product.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution and taxane feeding in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the missing C-1β hydroxylation but also generates rearranged side products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Proof-of-function T1βH, but less preferred than T1βH-686 for production engineering because of lower desirable product yield and side-product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; NMR

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxygenated taxane branch toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 1β-hydroxylated products including baccatin VI (11-Bz) and 10-Bz-related advanced intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses C-1β hydroxylation of advanced oxygenated taxanes to furnish late intermediates on the baccatin III route.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct late intermediates toward end product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T1βH-686, a second 2-ODD in module 2, functioned as a taxane 1β-hydroxylase. 2. It generated baccatin VI (11-Bz) and advanced pathway products when supplied with upstream gene sets. 3. It produced significantly higher levels of the desired 2α-O-benzoylated product 10-Bz than T1βH-184, so it was selected for subsequent pathway reconstitutions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares 72% identity with T1βH-184 but has more favorable product specificity for engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression and feeding assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS comparison with standards
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May still display product multiplicity depending on substrate context, but is more favorable than T1βH-184.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution and taxane feeding in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes productive C-1β hydroxylation and was the preferred enzyme for baccatin III pathway completion.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred T1βH for heterologous baccatin III/Taxol engineering due to higher desired product accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late deprotection/deacetylation branch toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Baccatin VI-like intermediates; 10-Bz/11-Bz pathway intermediates bearing C-9 acetyl group
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Deacetylated intermediate enabling 9DHAB formation and T9ox action
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses the C-9 O-acetyl group from late taxane intermediates as part of stepwise deacetylation toward baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream oxidation to end product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DeAc898 was identified as one of two deacetylases that stepwise remove extra acetyl groups from baccatin VI-like intermediates. 2. Pathway assembly showed that this enzyme is a prerequisite for T9ox activity. 3. It was therefore renamed taxane 9α-O-deacetylase (T9dA).
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports the model that extra acetylations serve as transient protecting groups during biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Feeding assays with baccatin VI/9DHAB-related standards in Nicotiana benthamiana; full pathway reconstitution; LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removing transient protective groups too early or without matched downstream steps could destabilize pathway flux; specific tradeoffs not directly measured.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of baccatin VI or 9DHAB to Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; transient pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables productive late-pathway deacetylation required for baccatin III biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway deprotection enzyme for engineering baccatin III production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late deprotection/deacetylation branch toward baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Late taxane intermediates bearing C-7 acetyl group, including baccatin VI-like compounds
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Further deacetylated intermediates including 9DHAB-related product pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses the C-7β O-acetyl group from late taxane intermediates during stepwise deacetylation en route to baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important late downstream step, not identified as sole prerequisite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Removes transient protective group required to furnish end product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DeAc1023 was identified as a deacetylase acting together with DeAc898 to remove two extra acetyl groups from late intermediates. 2. Stepwise deacetylation yielded a product matching the 9DHAB standard. 3. The enzyme was renamed taxane 7β-O-deacetylase (T7dA).
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports transient-protecting-group model in Taxol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Feeding assays with baccatin VI in Nicotiana benthamiana; full pathway reconstitution; LC-MS/MS and MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of baccatin VI or 9DHAB to Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; transient pathway reconstitution
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Removes a transient C-7 acetyl group required to finalize the baccatin III scaffold.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required late deacetylase for complete baccatin III engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal oxidation branch to baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C-9 position of late taxane intermediates, presumably forming the C-9 ketone required for baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for baccatin III formation in combination with deacetylases
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Baccatin III is the industrial precursor for the anti-cancer drug Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 2-ODD candidate was identified as taxane C-9 oxidase (T9ox) capable of oxidizing 9DHAB with loss of two protons, consistent with ketone formation. 2. Combining T9ox with T9dA and T7dA in the 14-gene upstream pathway generated abundant baccatin III. 3. Structural confirmation by NMR and MS/MS verified completion of de novo baccatin III biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Independently reported by another group; dropout analysis suggested baccatin III could still be detected without T9ox, implying partial compensation by host/endogenous or multifunctional activities under their system, but productive pathway includes T9ox.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Feeding assays with 9DHAB; transient reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS; purification and NMR confirmation against standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus needles; heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate identified from Taxol expression modules
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding of 9DHAB; transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of baccatin III by introducing the required C-9 ketone.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal oxidation enzyme for heterologous baccatin III production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS/MS; NMR

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates β-phenylalanine as a CoA thioester for BAPT-mediated side-chain installation onto baccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing side-chain activation step for production of 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Late paclitaxel precursor enabling progress toward the anti-cancer drug Taxol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies activated side-chain precursor required for direct late product formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Previously reported PCL candidates failed in the authors' Nicotiana system. 2. AAE-867.5, identified from Taxol expression modules, functioned as the missing β-phenylalanine-CoA ligase. 3. Co-expression of AAE-867.5 with PAM and BAPT plus fed or de novo synthesized baccatin III produced 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel, representing the first de novo biosynthesis of this late paclitaxel precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Resolved failure of previously proposed acyl-activating enzymes in this expression system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Candidate prioritization from mpXsn modules; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; feeding and de novo pathway assays; LC-MS detection of product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Heterologous production in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Prominent AAE candidate in Taxol expression modules
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression system; promoter type NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient pathway reconstitution in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; feeding of baccatin III
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables side-chain installation pathway to produce 3'-N-debenzoyl-2'-deoxypaclitaxel.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme for extending baccatin III engineering to late paclitaxel precursor production.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: snRNA-seq/mpXsn, GEO: GSE292840; metabolomics by LC-MS
